Module com.example._0tazo
Package com.example._0tazo.model

Class Game

java.lang.Object
com.example._0tazo.model.Game
public class Game extends Object
Orchestrates the Cincuentazo game: manages player order, turn progression, player elimination, and the win condition.

The table state (sum and top card) is delegated to Table. JavaFX dependencies (threads, timelines, UI updates) belong exclusively in the controller â€” this class is pure Java and fully testable with JUnit.

Typical usage from the controller:


 Game game = new Game(2);           // human + 2 machines
 game.setup();                      // deal hands and place first table card

 // Human turn â€” index comes from a card click in the UI:
 game.humanPlayCard(clickedIndex);
 game.currentPlayerDrawCard();
 game.nextTurn();

 // Computer turn â€” fully automatic:
 game.computerTakeTurn();
 game.nextTurn();
See Also:

    Constructor
    Description
    Game(int machineCount)
    Constructs a new game with one human player and the specified number of computer players (1, 2, or 3).

    void

    Makes the current player draw a card from the deck.

  • Field Details

    • players

      private final LinkedList<IPlayer> players
      Ordered list of players still in the game. LinkedList allows O(1) removal of eliminated players.

    • deck

      private final Deck deck
      The shared deck used by all players.

    • table

      private final Table table
      The table â€” owns the sum and the discard pile view.

    • currentPlayerIndex

      private int currentPlayerIndex
      Index into players pointing to the current player.

    • gameOver

      private boolean gameOver
      Whether the game has ended.

  • Constructor Details

    • Game

      public Game(int machineCount)
      Constructs a new game with one human player and the specified number of computer players (1, 2, or 3).
      Parameters:
      machineCount - the number of computer players (1â€“3)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if machineCount is not between 1 and 3

  • Method Details

    • setup

      public void setup() throws GameException
      Prepares the game: deals 4 cards to each player and places the initial card on the table to set the starting sum.
      Throws:
      GameException - if the deck runs out of cards during setup

    • humanPlayCard

      public void humanPlayCard(int cardIndex) throws GameException
      Processes the human player's chosen card.
      Parameters:
      cardIndex - the index of the card in the human player's hand (0â€“3)
      Throws:
      GameException.InvalidTurnException - if it is not the human player's turn
      GameException.PlayerEliminatedException - if the human player is eliminated
      GameException.SumExceededException - if the card would exceed 50
      GameException - if the card index is invalid

    • computerTakeTurn

      public void computerTakeTurn() throws GameException
      Executes a full computer player turn automatically: chooses the best card, plays it, and draws a replacement card. If the computer has no legal move it is eliminated immediately.
      Throws:
      GameException.InvalidTurnException - if it is the human player's turn
      GameException - if any deck or player error occurs

    • currentPlayerDrawCard

      public void currentPlayerDrawCard() throws GameException
      Makes the current player draw a card from the deck. Called by the controller after the human player plays a card.
      Throws:
      GameException - if the deck is empty or the player is eliminated

    • checkCurrentPlayerElimination

      public void checkCurrentPlayerElimination() throws GameException
      Checks whether the current player has any legal move and eliminates them automatically if not. The controller should call this at the start of every turn before enabling UI interaction.
      Throws:
      GameException - if an error occurs during elimination

    • nextTurn

      public void nextTurn()
      Advances the turn to the next active player. Marks the game as over if only one player remains.

    • eliminateCurrentPlayer

      public void eliminateCurrentPlayer() throws GameException
      Eliminates the current player: sends their cards to the deck, removes them from the list, and adjusts the turn index.
      Throws:
      GameException - if the player is already eliminated

    • getCurrentPlayer

      public IPlayer getCurrentPlayer()
      Returns:
      the player whose turn it currently is

    • getWinner

      public IPlayer getWinner()
      Returns:
      the last remaining player if the game is over, otherwise null

    • isGameOver

      public boolean isGameOver()
      Returns:
      true if the game has ended

    • getTableSum

      public int getTableSum()
      Returns:
      the current table sum

    • getTableCard

      public Card getTableCard()
      Returns:
      the top card on the table, or null if none

    • getDeckSize

      public int getDeckSize()
      Returns:
      the number of cards remaining in the main deck

    • getTable

      public Table getTable()
      Returns:
      the Table instance

    • getDeck

      public Deck getDeck()
      Returns:
      the shared Deck

    • getPlayers

      public LinkedList<IPlayer> getPlayers()
      Returns:
      a snapshot of active players in turn order
